mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW Z4 sDrive35i / 35is engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Z4 N54 engine typically costs £4,500–£11,000 supply only depending on the variant. sDrive35i units sit at the lower end (£4,500–£9,500), while sDrive35is units run £5,000–£11,000. Fitting adds £900–£2,000 depending on workshop location. Rebuilt N54 units for the E89 chassis are available with 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW Z4 sDrive35i / 35is engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N54 is a legendary engine among enthusiasts — it's one of the most capable and sought-after engines in the marketplace. However, it's not without its faults. Common issues include HPFP failure, fuel injector failure, wastegate rattle, oil leaks, and carbon build-up. The 35is adds more stress with higher boost and an overboost function, which can accelerate wear on these components. With proper maintenance and preventative upgrades, the N54 can be a reliable and rewarding engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW Z4 sDrive35i / 35is?

acceptedAnswer.text: HPFP failure (long cranking, rough idle, loss of power), fuel injector failure (rough idle, misfires), turbocharger wastegate rattle (rattling noise, boost loss), oil leaks from valve cover and oil filter housing gaskets, and carbon build-up on intake valves. The HPFP and injectors are the most expensive and common failures.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW Z4 sDrive35i / 35is engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% BMW scrap threshold: if your Z4 is worth £10,000 and a recon engine is £6,500 (65%), it's borderline. If the vehicle is a tired £8,000 example, the same engine exceeds 70% — get a scrap valuation first. The Z4's classic roadster status and the N54's tuning potential often justify replacement spend if the rest of the car is in good condition.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW Z4 sDrive35i / 35is engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The sDrive35i uses the N54B30A or N54B30O0 (306 bhp), while the sDrive35is uses the N54B30TO (335–340 bhp with overboost). The Z4 E89 was produced from 2009 to 2016. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the HPFP, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The HPFP typically requires replacement rather than repair (£500–£1,500). BMW issued multiple recalls covering HPFP failures on N54-equipped vehicles. Check whether your Z4 has had the recall work completed. Even with updated pumps, failures can still occur. If you experience long cranking or loss of power, have the HPFP inspected immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW Z4 sDrive35i / 35is Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market rates. Rebuilt N54 units for the E89 chassis are available with 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. A typical engine replacement cost in the UK ranges between £3,067 and £6,105 or more, with reconditioned engines typically costing £1,200–£2,200 supply only and total installed costs around £1,800–£2,800 for simpler engines — N54-specific costs are significantly higher due to complexity.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Z4 N54 engine typically takes 14–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The tight engine bay and complex twin-turbo layout add significant labour time. The 35is DCT transmission and overboost system add further complexity.

problems.title: High-Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Failure (N54)
problems.symptoms: Long cranking before starting, rough idle, loss of power, engine warning light, intermittent stalling, backfiring. In certain cases, the driver may experience reduced engine performance in a Safe Mode.
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–80,000 miles — premature failures were widespread, leading to multiple recalls.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, the vehicle may fail to start or go into limp mode.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The N54's HPFP is its most infamous weak point. BMW faced class-action lawsuits over defective HPFPs. The pump seems to have an abnormally high rate of failure. Symptoms include long-crank engine starting times. BMW issued multiple recalls covering the HPFP on N54-equipped vehicles. Check whether your Z4 has had the recall work completed. Even with updated pumps, failures can still occur. If you experience any starting issues or loss of power, have the HPFP inspected promptly.

problems.title: Fuel Injector Failure (N54)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, misfires, decreased performance, poor fuel economy, engine warning light, fuel smell in the oil.
problems.typicalMileage: 50,000–100,000 miles — earlier injectors (Index 10 or lower) are most susceptible.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, misfires can damage catalytic converters.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: N54 fuel injectors are piezo-electric injectors that have more in common with diesel injection than regular gas injectors. The main failure on earlier injectors is clogging and foreign debris restricting flow. On early N54 injectors, there's a filter that's known to melt at high temperatures. BMW redesigned these injectors numerous times — updated Index 12 injectors are the current solution. Any injector marked ten or lower in the same cylinder bank will require all the injectors in the bank to be replaced. A full set of Index 12 injectors is a common preventative upgrade on N54 cars.

problems.title: Turbocharger Wastegate Rattle (N54)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the engine bay, particularly on cold start or deceleration, turbo lag, reduced performance, check engine light.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — the wastegate bushings wear over time.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; if ignored, boost pressure drops and performance suffers.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if rattling is present.

problems.recommendation: Wastegate rattle is a common complaint on the N54. The wastegate bushings wear, causing rattling and reduced boost control. While not immediately catastrophic, it can lead to reduced performance and eventual turbo failure if ignored. Some specialists offer wastegate actuator repair kits as a cost-effective alternative to full turbo replacement. For the 35is, the higher boost pressure from the overboost function accelerates wastegate wear.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Z4 sDrive35i / 35is — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Essential repair — vehicle may be undriveable without it
Replace all six with Index 12 units — coding required
Adjust or rebuild — cheaper than full turbo replacement
Common maintenance — repair when symptoms appear
Full engine replacement required if bottom-end or valvetrain fails

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Z4 sDrive35i / 35is? Check These First
buyIf: * HPFP recall completed or recently replaced — the most critical N54 issue
* Index 12 injectors fitted — the updated injectors are far more reliable
* No wastegate rattle on cold start or deceleration
* Full service history with regular oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles)
avoidIf: * Long cranking on startup — HPFP failure imminent
* Rough idle or misfires — injector failure is likely
* Rattling from the turbo area — wastegate rattle
* Oil leaks from valve cover or oil filter housing — common but costly to fix
* Poor service history — the N54 is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Z4 sDrive35i and 35is are among the most exciting roadsters BMW has ever built — the N54 engine is a tuner's dream with forged internals from the factory. However, it comes with a well-earned reputation for reliability issues. The HPFP, injectors, and wastegate are the three biggest concerns. The 35is adds even more stress on these components with its higher boost and overboost function. With the 70% BMW scrap threshold, a £7,000 recon on a £10,000 Z4 is 70% — borderline. On a sub-£8,000 example, think carefully. The N54's tuning potential and the Z4's classic roadster status often justify replacement spend if the rest of the car is in good condition.

cta.label: → Compare Z4 sDrive35i / 35is engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW Z4 sDrive35i / 35is (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for long cranking (HPFP failure) or rattling (wastegate rattle)
* Idle Quality — Any rough idle or misfires? (injector failure)
* Service History — HPFP recalls completed? Index 12 injectors fitted?
* Carbon Cleaning — Has walnut blasting been performed? (recommended every 60,000–80,000 miles)
* Oil Leaks — Check around valve cover and oil filter housing
* Turbo Check — Any whistling, smoke, or power loss?
* DCT Check — For 35is, check for smooth gear changes — DCT issues are expensive
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
